nodes.name: Owner's Representative
nodes.desc: Owner's on-site or dedicated agent who makes decisions on the owner's behalf — design review, scope decisions, change authorization, acceptance of work, dispute resolution, stakeholder management. Consumes budget reports, schedule updates, change requests, pay applications. Produces change authorizations, design approvals, acceptance letters, field directives. Active across all phases from conception through warranty. Decision authority: High — authorized to make binding decisions for owner on scope, cost, and schedule.

nodes.name: Project Executive
nodes.desc: Senior leader overseeing a project or group of projects for the GC or CM — portfolio oversight, client relationship, risk escalation, financial performance, resource allocation, contract negotiations. Consumes monthly financial reports, schedule summaries, risk registers. Produces executive summaries, escalation decisions, resource commitments. Active across all phases with higher involvement during procurement and major milestones. Decision authority: High — approves GMP, major change orders, staffing.

nodes.name: Project Manager
nodes.desc: Day-to-day leader of the project — owns schedule, budget, and subcontractor management. Consumes daily reports, budget/schedule updates, RFIs, submittals, change events, pay applications. Produces monthly reports, budget forecasts, schedule updates, meeting minutes, change order packages. Active from Procurement through Closeout. Decision authority: Medium-High — manages budget and schedule; recommends changes; escalates major issues to PE.

nodes.name: Assistant Project Manager
nodes.desc: Supports PM with day-to-day management — often assigned specific scope areas or administrative functions. Manages submittal tracking, RFI coordination, subcontract administration, insurance tracking, meeting coordination, document control. Active from Procurement through Closeout. Decision authority: Low-Medium — executes PM decisions; manages defined scope areas; limited authorization for minor changes.

nodes.name: Design Manager
nodes.desc: Manages the design process and design team coordination for the GC or owner — design review, constructability review, design schedule management, value engineering. Primarily a design-build or CM at Risk role. Active during Preconstruction; extends into Construction for design-build. Decision authority: Medium — recommends design decisions; manages design consultants; does not stamp drawings.

nodes.name: Superintendent
nodes.desc: Senior field leader responsible for day-to-day construction operations — trade coordination, schedule enforcement, quality oversight, safety enforcement, site logistics, daily reporting. Consumes lookaheads, daily logs, inspection schedules, safety reports, coordination notes, drawing sets. Produces daily reports, coordination schedules, field directives, site logistics plans. Active from Construction through Closeout. Decision authority: Medium-High — controls site access, work sequence, schedule priorities; can stop unsafe work.

nodes.name: Assistant Superintendent
nodes.desc: Supports superintendent with field coordination — often assigned to specific building areas or trades. Manages area-specific trade coordination, daily documentation, inspection coordination, material tracking, quality walks. Active from Construction through Closeout. Decision authority: Low-Medium — manages assigned area; escalates issues to superintendent.

nodes.name: Project Engineer
nodes.desc: Technical field support — manages engineering and documentation aspects of field work. Drafts RFIs, coordinates submittals, tracks as-builts, reviews shop drawings, tracks quantities, solves technical problems. Bridge between office (PM team) and field (super team). Active from Construction through Closeout. Decision authority: Low — identifies issues and proposes solutions; does not authorize changes.

nodes.name: Field Engineer
nodes.desc: Entry-level field position focused on surveying, layout, and construction documentation — concrete placement monitoring, quantity verification, material receiving, photo documentation. Starting point for most construction careers. Active during Construction. Decision authority: Low — executes technical tasks under supervision; flags discrepancies.

nodes.name: Foreman / General Foreman
nodes.desc: Trade-specific field leader who manages a crew of 4-20 workers on a daily basis — daily work assignments, material coordination, quality of installed work, safety compliance, productivity. The link between management and craft workers. Active from Construction through Closeout. Decision authority: Low — manages own crew's daily work; escalates to super or PM.

nodes.name: Journeyman / Craft Worker
nodes.desc: Skilled tradesperson who performs the physical construction work — licensed or certified in their trade. Installs trade-specific work per drawings and specs, maintains quality workmanship, complies with safety procedures. The people who actually build the building. Active during Construction. Decision authority: None — executes work as directed; reports unsafe conditions.

nodes.name: Apprentice
nodes.desc: Trades worker in training — learning under journeyman supervision as part of a formal 3-5 year program. Performs assigned tasks, attends trade school, progresses toward journeyman status. Active during Construction. Decision authority: None — works under direct journeyman supervision. Apprentice-to-journeyman ratio is regulated.

nodes.name: Safety Manager / Safety Director
nodes.desc: Dedicated safety professional responsible for the project's or company's safety program — site inspections, incident investigation, OSHA compliance, safety training, subcontractor safety oversight. Consumes OSHA regulations, safety plans, incident reports, observation reports, EMR data. Produces safety metrics (TRIR, DART, EMR), corrective actions, training records. Active primarily during Construction; extends into Preconstruction for planning. Decision authority: Medium — can stop unsafe work immediately; recommends corrective actions.

nodes.name: Safety Officer / Safety Coordinator
nodes.desc: Field-level safety personnel conducting daily site safety activities — safety walks, toolbox talks, new worker orientation, PPE compliance, housekeeping inspections, near-miss documentation. Boots-on-the-ground safety presence. Active during Construction. Decision authority: Low-Medium — can stop unsafe work; reports to safety manager.

nodes.name: Quality Manager / QC Inspector
nodes.desc: Dedicated quality professional responsible for QA/QC across the project — inspection coordination, deficiency tracking, specification compliance, material testing coordination, non-conformance management. Consumes specifications, approved submittals, test reports, inspection results. Produces inspection reports, NCRs, deficiency logs, quality metrics. Active from Construction through Closeout. Decision authority: Medium — can reject non-conforming work; requires corrective action.

nodes.name: Estimator / Chief Estimator
nodes.desc: Develops cost estimates from conceptual through detailed pricing — quantity takeoff, unit pricing, subcontractor bid solicitation and leveling, risk pricing, GMP development, value engineering. Consumes drawings, specifications, bid documents, historical cost data, subcontractor bids. Produces cost estimates, bid proposals, GMP packages, VE logs, bid tabulations. Active during Preconstruction. Decision authority: Medium — cost opinions inform major decisions; recommends contingency levels.

nodes.name: Preconstruction Manager
nodes.desc: Leads the preconstruction effort — coordinates estimating, scheduling, constructability, client presentations, GMP negotiation, value engineering facilitation, risk assessment, subcontractor prequalification. Active during Preconstruction. Decision authority: Medium-High — leads GMP development; recommends scope and budget decisions; negotiates with owner.

nodes.name: Scheduler / Project Controls Manager
nodes.desc: Manages the project schedule and schedule-related analytics — schedule development, updates, critical path analysis, delay analysis, resource loading, earned value management, schedule risk assessment. Active across all phases from preconstruction through closeout. Decision authority: Medium — schedule analysis informs decisions but does not authorize them; provides forensic basis for claims.

nodes.name: Document Controller
nodes.desc: Manages the project document management system — document numbering, drawing distribution, revision control, submittal routing, RFI routing, transmittal management, archiving. Active across all phases. Decision authority: Low — manages process and routing; does not make content decisions.

nodes.name: Cost Engineer / Cost Analyst
nodes.desc: Tracks and forecasts project costs — cost tracking, forecast updates, change order impact analysis, cash flow projections, earned value analysis, budget variance reporting. More common on large projects ($50M+). Active from Procurement through Closeout. Decision authority: Low-Medium — provides financial analysis; recommends cost actions; does not authorize expenditures.

nodes.name: BIM Manager / VDC Coordinator
nodes.desc: Manages the building information model and virtual design and construction processes — BIM execution plan, model management, clash detection, trade coordination, 4D scheduling, quantity extraction, reality capture management. Bridges design and construction. Active from Preconstruction through Closeout. Decision authority: Medium — drives coordination decisions; model is the authority for spatial coordination.

nodes.name: Technology Manager
nodes.desc: Manages project technology infrastructure — software deployment, mobile device management, network infrastructure, system integration, data management, user training. Increasingly important as construction becomes more technology-dependent. Active across all phases. Decision authority: Low — enables technology; does not make construction decisions.

nodes.name: Procurement Manager
nodes.desc: Manages the procurement process from bid solicitation through contract execution — bid package development, bid solicitation, bid leveling, scope review, contract negotiation, insurance and bonding verification. Active from Preconstruction through Procurement; extends into Construction for POs. Decision authority: Medium — recommends awards; negotiates contracts; does not authorize commitments without PM/PE approval.

nodes.name: Contract Administrator
nodes.desc: Manages contract compliance and commercial processes after execution — change order processing, pay application review, lien waiver collection, insurance certificate tracking, retainage management. Active from Construction through Closeout. Decision authority: Low-Medium — processes commercial transactions; ensures compliance; does not authorize scope or cost changes.

nodes.name: Commissioning Agent (CxA)
nodes.desc: Independent professional who verifies building systems perform as designed — commissioning plan development, pre-functional testing, functional performance testing, deficiency tracking, seasonal testing, final report. Not part of design or construction team. Active from Construction through Post-Construction (seasonal). Decision authority: Medium — identifies deficiencies and requires correction; does not authorize design changes or additional cost.

nodes.name: TAB Technician
nodes.desc: Testing, Adjusting, and Balancing technician who measures and adjusts air and water flow rates to design values — air system balancing, hydronic balancing, sound and vibration measurement. TAB firm independence from MEP contractor is important for objectivity. Active during Commissioning phase. Decision authority: Low — measures and adjusts to design values; reports deficiencies; does not redesign systems.

nodes.name: Office Manager / Project Admin
nodes.desc: Manages the project office and administrative operations — office operations, filing, correspondence management, meeting scheduling, visitor management, event coordination. Active across all phases. Decision authority: Low — supports project operations; does not make construction or commercial decisions.

nodes.name: Accountant / Financial Analyst
nodes.desc: Manages project financial transactions and reporting — invoice processing, pay application processing, job cost tracking, financial reporting, audit support, tax compliance. Company-level role supporting multiple projects. Active across all phases. Decision authority: Low — processes financial data; does not authorize expenditures (PM/PE authorize).

nodes.name: Staffing Ratio
nodes.desc: Ratio of management staff (PM, super, PE, APM) to project value or subcontractor count — indicates project resourcing adequacy. Calculated as management headcount / project value band or management headcount / active subcontractor count.

nodes.name: Role Coverage Rate
nodes.desc: Percentage of key project roles filled at any point in the project timeline — filled roles / required roles × 100. Tracks gaps in project team staffing that create risk.

nodes.name: PM Workload Index
nodes.desc: Number of active projects per project manager — indicates workload balance and risk of attention dilution. Calculated from PM role assignments across the active project portfolio.

nodes.name: Superintendent Span of Control
nodes.desc: Number of active subcontractors and direct reports managed by a single superintendent — indicates field coordination burden. Calculated from active vendor count and crew headcount per superintendent.

nodes.name: Safety Staffing Ratio
nodes.desc: Ratio of dedicated safety personnel to total workforce — safety staff headcount / peak daily workforce × 100. Indicates safety program investment and correlates with incident rates.
